title: iris hartwegii subsp. australis
intro: Iris hartwegii subsp. australis is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Iris hartwegii plant, a type of flowering plant in the iris family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which may help to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as support immune function. In herbal preparations, Iris hartwegii subsp. australis is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to support skin health and wound healing.
